commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.7.9: the anatomy plate — annotation callouts land on a held specimen word

Recast the rejected POC annotate scene (a vague gradient terrain with
chat-bubble pins narrating 'Labels arrive as you scroll…') into THE ANATOMY
PLATE: the type designer's test word 'handgloves' held still in serif on its
four type-metric rules (ascender / x-height / baseline / descender), then a
draughtsman's callout LANDS for each glyph part — a target ring on the feature,
a thin leader to a tracked-mono term in the margin.

- Its OWN mechanic: static labels pinning onto ONE held still — no travelling
  boundary (wipe), no radial knockout (mask), and deliberately NO self-drawing
  stroke (draw): each callout settles in via opacity+lift. The satellite-
  annotation form turned into the specimen book's anatomy plate.
- Honest content replaces the meta narration: real anatomy terms, true one-line
  definitions, and an identity caption ('handgloves — set in Georgia') at rest.
- ONE scalar (shown) drives which callouts are on AND the live N/total readout
  [LAW:one-source-of-truth]; the metric-rule y's derive from one font-size +
  baseline, never hand-placed.
- Wears the shared specimen crop-mark frame (positive inset, the full-bleed
  convention); whole plate is one SVG so a leader provably touches its target.
- Serif face set in JS (the quoted-family inline trap); callouts land on n.w
  (dwell beat) so the word reads whole first. Pure scroll-driven, no new WAAPI.

Verified in Chrome at 810px across the node's range (~69346–72080px): 0/7
dwell, mid, 7/7 — every ring lands on its feature, zero console messages.

mit-styling-aj1.7.8: the proof — a proofing blade pulls a draft into the specimen (#31)

Recast the wipe node from a generic pink/orange gradient with a glowing white
divider into THE PROOF: a constant-speed letterpress proofing blade that sweeps
a loose grey DRAFT of a word into the tightly-kerned, jade-inked finished
SPECIMEN on a baseline rule, with a jade wet-ink edge trailing the blade. A
linear travelling boundary between two full states — deliberately distinct from
mask's radial knockout.

- ONE scalar --s on the scene root drives BOTH CSS consumers (the specimen
  layer's clip AND the blade position) AND the readout [LAW:one-source-of-truth];
  the blade can never leave the reveal edge, the number can never disagree.
- bmf feedback honored ('before/after is boring — longer AND a twist'): longer
  via tempo 'movement' (the palette's longest breath); the twist is the
  print-proof reading — grey->jade ink, loose->tight tracking, baseline rule
  drawing in behind a wet-ink blade.
- Sweep is LINEAR in n.w with beat:'dwell' so the draft holds still through the
  read-beat, then a constant-speed blade (the honest press metaphor) stays
  aligned with the linear stepIdx captions (no easeOut front-load/desync).
- background:inherit makes the specimen layer opaque so the wipe occludes the
  wider loose-tracked draft instead of ghosting its outer letters through.
- Wears the shared specimen frame (8-strip crop-mark pseudo, positive inset),
  tracked-mono catalogue label, matte jade chrome, no bloom.
- Honest readout textContent-direct (no setText flicker); pure scroll-driven,
  no WAAPI -> REDUCE_MOTION via the global keyframe kill.

Verified in Chrome at 810px across the node's full active range: zero page
console errors (only the benign favicon 404), clip/blade/readout/captions all
agree, draft holds through the dwell, finished specimen stands clean.
